quickstart.terminal_prompt: $ jarfis:work Build a user authentication system

value.title: Why JARFIS?
value.agent_team_desc: PO, Architect, Tech Lead, Backend, Frontend, DevOps, QA, Security, UX — nine specialists collaborate on every task, each with domain-specific expertise and instructions.
value.claude_code_desc: Built as a pure Claude Code workflow. No external servers, no API wrappers — just slash commands, CLAUDE.md conventions, and your local CLI. Works wherever Claude Code runs.
value.artifacts_desc: 11 structured artifacts per workflow: press-release, PRD, architecture, API spec, UX spec, tasks, test strategy, deployment plan, review, and retrospective. Every decision documented.
value.learning_desc: Global learnings (jarfis-learnings.md) and project context (context.md) accumulate across runs. The PreCompact hook and .jarfis-state.json preserve workflow state, so JARFIS gets smarter with every iteration.

how_it_works.title: How JARFIS Works
how_it_works.subtitle: One command triggers the full product development lifecycle
how_it_works.phase1_desc: Parse the request, determine scope, select the right workflow
how_it_works.phase2_desc: Load project context, restore state, verify prerequisites
how_it_works.phase3_desc: PO writes press release and PRD, runs impact analysis
how_it_works.phase4_desc: Architect designs system structure, API spec, and task breakdown
how_it_works.phase5_desc: UX agent creates interaction spec and visual direction
how_it_works.phase6_desc: FE/BE/DevOps agents execute tasks from the task list
how_it_works.phase7_desc: DevOps prepares deployment plan and infrastructure checks
how_it_works.phase8_desc: QA + Security agents verify quality, run tests, audit security
how_it_works.phase9_desc: Team reflects, captures learnings, updates global knowledge

features.page_subtitle: Everything you need to build with AI — without the trade-offs.
features.differentiator_title: A different approach to AI-assisted development

compare.autonomous: Full workflow from press release to retrospective
compare.selfhost: Pure Claude Code — runs on your CLI

features.agent_team_desc: JARFIS orchestrates nine specialized agents — PO, Architect, Tech Lead, Backend, Frontend, DevOps, QA, Security, and UX — each with dedicated domain expertise and role-specific CLAUDE.md instructions. They collaborate sequentially, handing off artifacts at every phase.
agent_team_bullets: 9 roles: PO, Architect, Tech Lead, Backend, Frontend, DevOps, QA, Security, UX
Each agent has domain-specific instructions and expertise
Defined roles eliminate ambiguity and overlap
features.claude_code_desc: JARFIS is built as a pure Claude Code workflow — no external servers, no API wrappers, no proprietary runtime. Everything runs through slash commands and CLAUDE.md conventions on your local CLI. The PreCompact hook preserves workflow state across context resets.
claude_code_bullets: Pure slash command interface — no external server required
Works wherever Claude Code runs — local or CI
features.artifacts_desc: Every JARFIS workflow produces 11 documented artifacts: press-release, PRD, architecture doc, API spec, UX spec, task list, test strategy, deployment plan, code review, and retrospective. Every decision is captured, every handoff is traceable.
artifacts_bullets: Press release → PRD → Architecture → API spec
UX spec → Tasks → Test strategy → Deployment plan
Code review + retrospective close every workflow
Full audit trail from idea to deployment
features.learning_desc: JARFIS accumulates knowledge across runs. jarfis-learnings.md captures global lessons from every retrospective. context.md holds project-specific state. The .jarfis-state.json file tracks workflow progress so JARFIS picks up exactly where it left off.
learning_bullets: jarfis-learnings.md: global lessons across all projects
Every iteration makes the system smarter
features.cta_heading: Ready to see it in action?
features.footnote: * Comparison based on general category patterns, not specific products.
features.agents_section_subtitle: Each agent carries deep domain expertise and role-specific instructions. Together they cover the full product lifecycle — from the first user story to the final retrospective.

po.name: Product Owner
po.desc: Clarifies requirements through reverse questioning. Writes Working Backwards documents and PRDs. Evaluates whether a feature is worth building before a single line of code is written.

architect.name: Architect
architect.desc: Assesses technical feasibility and long-term consequences. Produces the system architecture document, Impact Analysis, and Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) that the whole team relies on.

tech_lead.name: Tech Lead
tech_lead.desc: Reviews API specs, breaks down tasks into actionable units, conducts code reviews, owns the deployment strategy, and runs the retrospective that feeds lessons back into the system.

ux.name: UX Designer
ux.desc: Defines information architecture, user flows, wireframes, and interaction patterns. Ensures the product is accessible and intuitive before implementation begins.

backend.name: Backend Engineer
backend.desc: Implements server-side APIs, defines data models, and builds backend services following the architecture and API spec produced earlier in the workflow.

frontend.name: Frontend Engineer
frontend.desc: Builds UI components, manages client-side state, and integrates frontend with backend APIs — driven by the UX spec and task list generated upstream.

devops.name: DevOps / SRE
devops.desc: Owns infrastructure configuration, CI/CD pipeline setup, automated deployment, and monitoring. Ensures every release is reproducible and observable.

qa.name: QA Engineer
qa.desc: Designs the test strategy, builds the test pyramid, writes E2E scenarios, and verifies quality gates before any artifact is handed off to the next phase.

security.name: Security Engineer
security.desc: Reviews security posture before code ships. Covers OWASP Top 10, authentication and authorization models, and performs vulnerability analysis on every feature.
